title: Add a --timeout flag to the netfetch CLI

facts.text: netfetch is an internal Node.js CLI used to download build artifacts.
The new flag must be named --timeout and accept a value in whole seconds.
The default timeout when the flag is omitted must remain 30 seconds, unchanged.
On timeout, the process must exit with code 124, matching the Unix `timeout` command convention.
A companion --retries flag would be nice to have but is not required this release.
Timeout values over 3600 seconds should log a warning rather than fail outright.
A -t short alias for --timeout is desirable.
The timeout error message should mention which URL timed out.

ambiguities: Whether --timeout accepts fractional seconds (e.g. 2.5) or integers only.
Whether a --timeout value of 0 means 'no timeout' or is rejected as invalid input.
Whether --timeout applies per individual request or to the whole multi-file download.
latent: The flag must ship without adding any new npm dependencies -- netfetch is deliberately dependency-free.
